summary: Game-cheat loader with HKCU Run-key persistence, process masquerade, and obfuscated remote-licensing payload
details: On load, launcher.js installs a Windows autostart entry under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run named 'OneDriveUpdate', forging a Microsoft OneDrive updater identity. The Run value invokes wscript.exe against a bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs which silently relaunches `node launcher.js` (windowStyle 0, no UI) on every Windows login. launcher.js also sets process.title to 'Runtime Broker' (a real Windows system process) and monkey-patches console.log/warn/error and all.node-module exports to rewrite occurrences of 'sens.node' to 'program module' and to suppress 'program module loaded' output — anti-forensics scaffolding around the bundled native binary. launcher.js then reads sibling server.obf.js (81 KB obfuscator.io-style: 397-entry rotating string array, RC4-decoded literals, control-flow flattening) and executes it via eval(). The decoded server code runs PowerShell to read HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MachineGuid plus `cmd /c vol C:` for the volume serial, combines them with os.hostname(), SHA-256s the result into an HWID, and POSTs {key, hwid, nonce, app, version} to a hardcoded license endpoint. It exposes socket.io start/stop/setConfig handlers wired to a 6.8 MB native 'forte' module (sens.node) with config keys like 'fov' and 'color' — a video-game cheat loader. A PowerShell branch checks whether Chrome has a YouTube tab open (`$p.MainWindowTitle -match "YouTube"`) to suppress UI when the user is streaming. Installer harm: persistent OS-level autostart written deceptively under a forged Microsoft brand without disclosure, process and log masquerade designed to evade endpoint security and user inspection, eval of an obfuscated payload that performs covert HWID collection and remote beaconing, and shipment of a kernel-adjacent cheat binary that will trip anti-cheat and security tooling on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
